name: Canakinumab did not prevent incident frailty in CANTOS
summary: A frailty post hoc analysis directly limits broad aging interpretation of IL-1beta inhibition.
statement: In a post hoc CANTOS analysis, random assignment to canakinumab did not reduce incident frailty over 5 years compared with placebo.
population_or_model: Stable adult CANTOS participants with atherosclerosis; median age 61 years in the frailty analysis
quantitative_note: Among 9,942 participants with baseline frailty-index data, incident frailty hazard ratio for any canakinumab dose versus placebo was 1.03 (95% CI 0.91 to 1.17; P=0.63).
caveats: Frailty was analyzed post hoc and the CANTOS cohort was not designed as a geroscience trial.
The null frailty result does not negate cardiovascular benefit, but it blocks a simple anti-aging interpretation.
Participants were post-myocardial-infarction adults rather than a broadly healthy older-adult cohort.
